sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
कथं भीष्ममहं संख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन।
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
kathaṃ bhīṣmam ahaṃ saṃkhye droṇaṃ ca madhusūdana
iṣubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāvarisūdana
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: How (katham) can I (aham) counter-attack (pratiyotsyāmi) with arrows (iṣubhiḥ) in battle (saṃkhye) Bhīṣma and Droṇa, who are worthy of worship (pūjārha), O killer of Madhu (madhusūdana), O destroyer of foes (arisūdana)?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences acute moral-identity conflict: the same elders he has revered as internalized parental figures now stand as military targets, creating an impossible double-bind between duty (kṣatriya code) and attachment (filial piety).
psychological_state: Moral-paralysis; ego-dystonic aggression blocked by superego injunctions; anticipatory survivor-guilt.
trigger_scenario: A junior surgeon ordered to operate on her beloved mentor who is now a terminal patient; cutting the flesh feels like attacking the one who taught her to heal.
anchor_text: Honor your duty even when it demands confronting those you revere.
